OPPOSITION PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FILED BY DEFENDANTS

DISCOVERY 610, 1) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. # 3.2: THE ANSWER IS INCOMPLETE. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE A COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSE WITHIN 21 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. #15.1: THE ANSWER IS INCOMPLETE. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SPECIFY THE WRITINGS FROM WHICH THE ANSWER MAY BE DERIVED OR ASCERTAINED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2030(F)(2) WITHIN 21 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT ANY OF THE DOCUMENTS INVOLVE COMMUNICATIONS IMPLICATING THE ATTORNEY - CLIENT OR ATTORNEY - WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGES, PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE A PRIVILEGE LOG. 2) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS IS DENIED AT TO REQUEST #1. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS: 6, 12 - 15, 20 - 23. SUBJECT TO THE PREVIOUS STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER, DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHIN 21 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. 3) THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTION 2023(C). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WARREN WOLFELD'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED -- AMENDMENT STATES A VALID CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR#5220.

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED. AS DRAFTED, THE SUBPOENA IS OVERBROAD AS THE REQUEST ENCOMPASSES PROPOSED AND FINAL SPECIAL VERDICT FORMS THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF THE PREVIOUS COURT ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL. ADDITIONALLY, THE SUBPOENA INCLUDES INFORMATION RELATING TO THE SUBSEQUENT EMPLOYER OF THE PLAINTIFF IN MILLER V. FDML. THE COURT ORDERS THAT COUNSEL FOR FDML MEET AND CONFER WITH COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO DETERMINE THE PROPER SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA. (610/BCHAN)
